chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C. P. No.S-410 of 2025 Date Order with Signature of Judge Hearing of case 1. For order on office objections No.1 to 5 a/w reply as at ‘A’. 2. For hearing of main case. 02.06.2025 Mr. Abdul Aziz, Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr. Ahmed Khan Khaskheli, AAG Sindh. DSP-Raza Mian on behalf of I.G. SDPO-Muhammad Aslam Joiya, District Korangi DSP- Abdul Rauf Nehrio, District Thatta DSP-Javed Ahmed, District SBA SHO-Zahoor Ahmed Noonari of Police Station B-Section, District Tando Muhammad Khan Inspector – Syed Irfan Ahmed SDI New Town, District East Karachi DSP/SDPO – Jawed Ahmed Daulatpur, District Nawabshah PI-Liaquat Sarki on behalf of SSP District Hyderabad PI-Syed Atif Iqbal of Police Station, District Mirpurkhas PI-Vijay Kumar, District Umarkot PI-Muhammad Nawaz, District Sujawal PI-Muhammad Amin Arain, District Kashmore PI-Sanaqat Ali, District Khairpur PI-Naveed Akbar Pirzada, District Ghotki PI-Aftab Memon, District Matiari PI-Ghulam Qadir, District Badin PI-Mocharo Mangrio, District Tharparkar PI-Hakim Ali Soomro of Police Station Quaidabad Malir Karachi PI-Riaz Hussain, District Central Karachi SIP-Syed Ahsan Ali on behalf of SSP West Karachi SIP- Adnan Ismat of Police Station Defence, South Karachi SIP-Barkat Ali, District Jacobabad SIP-Muhammad Tariq of Police Station Korangi, Karachi SIP-Muhammad Nawaz Sanjrani, District Shikarpur SIP-Shabir Ahmed Malik, District Tando Allahyar SIP-Hammad Ali Shaikh, District Sanghar SIP-Muhammad Bachal Naich, District Sukkur SIP-Nizam Ali Mehran, District Larkana ASI-Asadullah Chandio, District Naushaheroferoze ASI-Nisar Ahmed Soomro, District Jamshoro ------------------------------- The petitioners through the instant petition have claimed the following reliefs: “It is, therefore, respectfully prayed that this Honorable Court may graciously be pleased to pass orders as under: a.

DSP-Raza Mian on behalf of I.G. SDPO-Muhammad Aslam Joiya, District Korangi DSP- Abdul Rauf Nehrio, District Thatta DSP-Javed Ahmed, District SBA SHO-Zahoor Ahmed Noonari of Police Station B-Section, District Tando Muhammad Khan Inspector – Syed Irfan Ahmed SDI New Town, District East Karachi DSP/SDPO – Jawed Ahmed Daulatpur, District Nawabshah PI-Liaquat Sarki on behalf of SSP District Hyderabad PI-Syed Atif Iqbal of Police Station, District Mirpurkhas PI-Vijay Kumar, District Umarkot PI-Muhammad Nawaz, District Sujawal PI-Muhammad Amin Arain, District Kashmore PI-Sanaqat Ali, District Khairpur PI-Naveed Akbar Pirzada, District Ghotki PI-Aftab Memon, District Matiari PI-Ghulam Qadir, District Badin PI-Mocharo Mangrio, District Tharparkar PI-Hakim Ali Soomro of Police Station Quaidabad Malir Karachi PI-Riaz Hussain, District Central Karachi SIP-Syed Ahsan Ali on behalf of SSP West Karachi SIP- Adnan Ismat of Police Station Defence, South Karachi SIP-Barkat Ali, District Jacobabad SIP-Muhammad Tariq of Police Station Korangi, Karachi SIP-Muhammad Nawaz Sanjrani, District Shikarpur SIP-Shabir Ahmed Malik, District Tando Allahyar SIP-Hammad Ali Shaikh, District Sanghar SIP-Muhammad Bachal Naich, District Sukkur SIP-Nizam Ali Mehran, District Larkana ASI-Asadullah Chandio, District Naushaheroferoze ASI-Nisar Ahmed Soomro, District Jamshoro ------------------------------- The petitioners through the instant petition have claimed the following reliefs: “It is, therefore, respectfully prayed that this Honorable Court may graciously be pleased to pass orders as under: a. To declare the act of the Respondents and the police performing duties throughout the province of Sindh in causing illegal interference and deterrence in the lawful trade and business of the petitioners, is illegal, unlawful and against all norms of law and amounts to causing harassment to the petitioners. b. Direct the Respondents throughout the province of Sindh to keep check and balance over the subordinate police officers/officials not to cause illegal interference for extraneous reasons in the smooth run of the lawful trade/business of the petitioners. c. Direct the Respondents to ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioners in future in carrying on the lawful business and trade of the petitioners.” It is very strange that all the SSPs of the Districts working in the Province of Sindh have been made party without making particular allegations against them of causing harassment. Today the entire Courtroom is full with the police officials from all districts of the Sindh Province. The respective replies filed by police officials are taken on record. This petition from the face of it appears to be an act of manipulation and harassment against the police officials as, who instead of performing their official duties, have been compelled to appear before this Court. The petitioners submit that they are doing their lawful business but the police officials are causing harassment against them without showing that any FIR in any of the police stations in Province of Sindh has been registered against them and such practice on the part of the petitioners is not appreciable at all. Since the police officials from all districts of Sindh have appeared on notices issued by this Court, therefore, in order to compensate them for the unnecessary travel to this Court, the petitioners are directed to pay the amount of Rs.5000/- to each of the police officials from all the districts except Karachi and they shall submit the amount within three weeks’ time before the Nazir of this Court and in case of failure their CNICs will be blocked and thereafter the deposited amount will be disbursed to the police officials who have attended the Court today.

The petitioners submit that they are doing their lawful business but the police officials are causing harassment against them without showing that any FIR in any of the police stations in Province of Sindh has been registered against them and such practice on the part of the petitioners is not appreciable at all. Since the police officials from all districts of Sindh have appeared on notices issued by this Court, therefore, in order to compensate them for the unnecessary travel to this Court, the petitioners are directed to pay the amount of Rs.5000/- to each of the police officials from all the districts except Karachi and they shall submit the amount within three weeks’ time before the Nazir of this Court and in case of failure their CNICs will be blocked and thereafter the deposited amount will be disbursed to the police officials who have attended the Court today. The petitioners are also directed to remain careful in future and they should file petition before this Court or any other forum by arraying the respondents against whom they claim a particular relief. Since the petitioners have claimed that the police is harassing them, the official respondents of Districts of Karachi are directed to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and not to cause any harassment against the petitioners, however, if the petitioners are found involved in any sort of illegal activities the official respondents would be at liberty to take action against them. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Asif
